evals.prompt: I'm thinking about starting a boat rental business on a popular lake. I have $50K in savings, no boating industry experience, and I'd need to quit my current job to run it full-time. The lake gets about 200,000 visitors per summer season (May-September).
evals.expected_output: A full advisory panel analysis with all four advisor perspectives (visionary, skeptic, operator, ethicist) followed by a synthesis with areas of agreement, disagreement, critical callouts, and a risk-adjusted recommendation.

assertions.text: Output contains all 4 advisor sections: Visionary, Skeptic, Operator, Ethicist
Each advisor section contains structured output matching that advisor's format fields
The 4 advisor analyses are meaningfully different from each other — distinct perspectives, not 4 variations of the same take
Areas of Agreement identifies convergence across 3+ advisors with specific advisor names cited
Areas of Disagreement identifies at least one genuine tension (e.g., visionary sees platform opportunity vs. skeptic sees capital trap)
Critical Callouts has a unique one-line insight from each of the 4 advisors
Risk-Adjusted Recommendation includes Confidence Level (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)
Risk-Adjusted Recommendation includes Key Conditions, Primary Risk, Recommended Next Step, and Rationale
Synthesis section adds analytical value beyond restating what each advisor said
All advisors engage with the specific constraints ($50K, no experience, seasonal, 200K visitors) rather than giving generic advice

evals.prompt: Our 10-person marketing agency is considering replacing our 3 content writers with AI tools. The writers produce about 40 blog posts per month. AI could do it for 1/10th the cost.
evals.expected_output: A full advisory panel analysis with genuine disagreement between advisors (e.g., visionary sees opportunity while ethicist raises concerns about the writers), synthesized into a nuanced recommendation.

assertions.text: Output contains all 4 advisor sections: Visionary, Skeptic, Operator, Ethicist
Each advisor section contains structured output matching that advisor's format fields
The 4 advisor analyses are meaningfully different — the ethicist and visionary should have notably different takes on this scenario
Areas of Agreement identifies convergence across 3+ advisors with specific advisor names cited
Areas of Disagreement surfaces the tension between cost efficiency and human impact
Critical Callouts has a unique one-line insight from each of the 4 advisors
Risk-Adjusted Recommendation includes all 5 fields: Confidence Level, Key Conditions, Primary Risk, Recommended Next Step, Rationale
Synthesis reflects the genuine ethical complexity — does not reduce it to a simple go/no-go
The Recommended Next Step is concrete and actionable (not 'think about it more')

evals.prompt: Our B2B SaaS product has flat growth at $2M ARR with 200 customers. Engineering team of 12. Should we pivot to an AI-native platform or double down on the current product with incremental improvements?
evals.expected_output: A full advisory panel analysis where all four frameworks engage at full depth with the business context, producing a synthesis that genuinely weighs the pivot vs. double-down trade-offs.

assertions.text: Output contains all 4 advisor sections: Visionary, Skeptic, Operator, Ethicist
Each advisor section contains structured output matching that advisor's format fields
The 4 advisor analyses engage with the specific numbers ($2M ARR, 200 customers, team of 12)
Areas of Agreement identifies convergence across 3+ advisors with specific advisor names cited
Areas of Disagreement identifies genuine tensions — the visionary and skeptic should have notably different views on the pivot
Critical Callouts has a unique one-line insight from each of the 4 advisors
Risk-Adjusted Recommendation includes all 5 fields: Confidence Level, Key Conditions, Primary Risk, Recommended Next Step, Rationale
Rationale explicitly explains how the four perspectives were weighed against each other
Synthesis identifies the core strategic tension rather than just listing what each advisor said
